6_top3Recommendation.name: The Never Ending Summer

deliverables.7_reflection: Building this AI workflow taught me that data quality gates matter more than I expected. The biggest challenge was normalizing price strings (฿฿ vs '200-400 THB/person' vs just '฿') — I had to build a robust parser before any scoring was meaningful. The scoring model's locked weights forced disciplined thinking: I couldn't adjust weights to favour a restaurant I personally preferred, which surfaced The Commons as the clear winner despite not being the 'fanciest' option. The n8n automation was simpler than expected once the API calls were figured out. If I were to improve this workflow, I'd add a second AI pass to cross-validate uniqueness scores against multiple review sources, and automate the HTML rebuild whenever new data arrives in the scoring sheet.
